exercises.title: Compare query plans
exercises.description: Run the same SQL query before and after adding an index and inspect the plan change.
exercises.expected_outcome: The indexed version should avoid a full table scan when the predicate is selective.

exercises.title: Inspect active sessions
exercises.description: Use the database shell to list active connections and identify long-running queries.
exercises.command: psql -c "select pid, state, query from pg_stat_activity;"
exercises.expected_outcome: You should see which sessions are active, idle, or waiting.

exercises.title: Measure seq scan vs index scan
exercises.description: Create a table, load data, and compare a range query with and without an index.
exercises.expected_outcome: The indexed range query should touch fewer rows or pages.

exercises.title: Observe WAL growth
exercises.description: Run a write-heavy workload and watch how the write-ahead log changes over time.
exercises.expected_outcome: WAL activity should increase as inserts and updates accumulate.

exercises.title: Build a log-structured key-value store
exercises.description: Append writes to a log, rebuild state on startup, and compact old segments.
exercises.expected_outcome: Reads should reconstruct the latest value while compaction reduces old data.

exercises.title: Benchmark range scans
exercises.description: Compare range queries on a table scan versus a B-tree-backed index.
exercises.expected_outcome: The indexed path should outperform the table scan for selective ranges.
